user_prompt: I thought we fixed the `Network.h` issue with `-I $PROJECT_PACKAGES_DIR/framework-arduinoespressif32/libraries/Network/src`?
agent_reply: Yep, that was the idea, but the path was stale/wrong for this install.
But the active framework being compiled is actually under the global package cache with a hashed folder name:
So the include flag was aiming at a directory that didn’t contain `Network.h`.
I replaced the hardcoded include with a small PlatformIO pre-script:
That script asks PlatformIO for the actual active `framework-arduinoespressif32` package dir and appends its `libraries/Network/src`.
Yep, that was the idea, but the path was stale/wrong for this install.
But the active framework being compiled is actually under the global package cache with a hashed folder name:
So the include flag was aiming at a directory that didn’t contain `Network.h`.
I replaced the hardcoded include with a small PlatformIO pre-script:
That script asks PlatformIO for the actual active `framework-arduinoespressif32` package dir and appends its `libraries/Network/src`.
